message:
feat(abi): eliminate glibc call-throughs, add re-entrancy guards, conditional no_mangle

Promote 5 more ABI families from GlibcCallThrough to Implemented
(termios, dirent, signal, socket, unistd) by routing through raw
SYS_* syscall entrypoints. This moves the parity count from 113/250
to 118/250 implemented symbols.

Key changes across all *_abi.rs modules:
- Replace `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` with `#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions),
  unsafe(no_mangle))]` to avoid symbol conflicts in test builds
- build.rs: skip version-script linker arg in debug/test builds
- macros.rs: update abi_fn! macro to use conditional no_mangle
- malloc_abi: add direct __libc_malloc/calloc/realloc/free symbol
  bindings with re-entrancy guard to prevent infinite recursion
- pthread_abi: add ThreadingPolicyGuard for re-entrant mutex safety,
  remove promote_unmanaged_default_mutex in favor of explicit init
- string_abi: add StringMembraneGuard re-entrancy protection and
  raw_memcpy_bytes/raw_memmove_bytes volatile byte-copy fallbacks
- socket_abi: route all socket ops through SYS_* raw syscalls
- termios_abi: route tc*/cf* through sys_ioctl and flag math
- dirent_abi: route opendir/closedir/readdir through sys_openat/
  sys_close/sys_getdents64
- signal_abi: route raise/kill through SYS_kill
- unistd_abi: route metadata/navigation/identity/link ops through
  raw syscall entrypoints (newfstatat, faccessat, *at variants)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
